# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Validator;
use GuzzleHttp\Client;

class PreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function getPreview(Request $request)
    {
        $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
            'url' => 'required|active_url'
        ]);

        if ($validator->fails()) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL'], 400);
        }

        $url = $request->input('url');

        try {
            $client = new Client();
            $response = $client->get($url, [
                'timeout' => 10.0,
                'headers' => [
                    'User-Agent' => 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/87.0.4280.88 Safari/537.36',
                ],
            ]);

            $html = (string) $response->getBody();

            // Simple parsing to get title and description
            preg_match('/<title>(.*?)<\/title>/i', $html, $titleMatches);
            preg_match('/<meta.*?name="description".*?content="(.*?)"/i', $html, $descriptionMatches);

            $preview = [
                'url' => $url,
                'title' => isset($titleMatches[1]) ? strip_tags($titleMatches[1]) : null,
                'description' => isset($descriptionMatches[1]) ? strip_tags($descriptionMatches[1]) : null,
            ];

            return response()->json($preview);

        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Unable to fetch content'], 500);
        }
    }
}
